questions.question_text: Colorado licensees are required to use forms approved by the:
questions.explanation: The Colorado Real Estate Commission approves standard forms for use in real estate transactions. Licensees are required to use these Commission-approved forms.

questions.question_text: A broker uses a contract form downloaded from the internet instead of the Commission-approved version. This is:
distractors: Permitted if the content is similar
Permitted with the seller's written consent
questions.explanation: Licensees must use Commission-approved forms. Using unauthorized forms — even if the content is similar — violates Commission rules.

questions.question_text: How often does the Commission update its approved forms?
questions.correct_answer: Regularly, as laws and practices change
distractors: Only once per decade at most
Only when the legislature mandates changes
Never — the approved forms are permanent
questions.explanation: Commission-approved forms are updated regularly to reflect changes in legislation, court decisions, and real estate practice. Brokers must use the current versions.

questions.question_text: Which is NOT a Commission-approved standard form?
distractors: Contract to Buy and Sell Residential
questions.explanation: Custom attorney-drafted contracts are not Commission-approved forms. The Commission's approved forms include the Contract to Buy and Sell (CBS1), the Exclusive Right-to-Sell Listing Contract (LC50), and the Exclusive Right-to-Buy Listing Contract (BC60) — Colorado's buyer-representation form.

questions.question_text: A broker is unsure whether a form version is current. They should check with the:
distractors: The title company handling the deal
questions.explanation: Current Commission-approved forms are available on the Division of Real Estate website. Brokers should always verify they are using the most current version.

questions.question_text: The Inspection Objection/Resolution addendum is used to:
questions.explanation: The Inspection Objection/Resolution forms are used after the inspection period to negotiate repairs, credits, or other remedies for issues found during inspection.

questions.question_text: Colorado's special Commission-approved foreclosure purchase contract (Colorado Foreclosure Protection Act) must be used when:
questions.correct_answer: An equity purchaser buys a residence in foreclosure from its owner
distractors: A bank sells one of its REO properties
Any buyer purchases at the public trustee auction
A lender assigns a loan before the foreclosure sale
questions.explanation: The CFPA (C.R.S. 6-1-1111 et seq.) covers 'equity purchasers' — buyers acquiring title to a residence in foreclosure other than to occupy it as their personal residence. The Commission approves a dedicated CBS (Colorado Foreclosure Protection Act) contract plus notice and cancellation forms for these sales. Bank-owned (REO) sales use the standard CBS, typically with the bank's own addenda.

questions.question_text: A short sale requires lender approval of the sale price. Which addendum addresses this?
questions.explanation: The Short Sale Addendum is used when the sale is contingent on the lender approving a price below the outstanding loan balance.

questions.question_text: A bank selling its REO property requires its own attorney-prepared addendum to the CBS. The broker may:
questions.correct_answer: Use it as a party-provided form, inserting only transaction-specific information
distractors: Not use it — only Commission-approved addenda are permitted
Use it only after Commission approval
Retype its terms into Additional Provisions verbatim
questions.explanation: Rule 7.1.C expressly permits brokers to use forms provided by a party to the transaction (such as a bank's REO addendum), completing only transaction-specific blanks. There is no Commission-approved addendum required for REO sales.

questions.question_text: Which addendum would be used if the buyer's inspection reveals a broken HVAC system?
questions.explanation: The Inspection Objection/Resolution forms are used to negotiate repairs or credits for issues discovered during the inspection, such as a broken HVAC system.

questions.question_text: The Counterproposal Form is used to:
questions.correct_answer: Modify terms of an existing offer
questions.explanation: The Counterproposal form changes specified terms of an offer while incorporating all its unmodified terms. Legally, a counterproposal is a REJECTION of the original offer and a new offer — once countered, the original offer can no longer be accepted.

questions.question_text: Closing Instructions in Colorado coordinate the transaction between:
questions.correct_answer: The parties and the title/closing company
distractors: The buyer and their lender only
The seller and the county recorder
questions.explanation: Standard Closing Instructions forms coordinate the transaction between all parties and the title or closing company, ensuring everyone is aligned on the closing process.

questions.question_text: Under the Colorado Contract to Buy and Sell, seller-financing documents should be prepared by:
questions.correct_answer: A licensed Colorado attorney or licensed mortgage loan originator
distractors: The listing broker using Commission forms
The seller, using the buyer's lender's forms
questions.explanation: The CBS warns that, unless exempt, financing provisions and documents should be prepared by a licensed Colorado attorney or licensed mortgage loan originator (CBS section 4.7). Brokers should not prepare, or advise the parties on the specifics of, seller-financing documents.

questions.question_text: In a seller-financed CBS transaction, the seller may terminate the contract if:
questions.correct_answer: The financing terms are unsatisfactory to the seller by the Seller or Private Financing Deadline
distractors: The buyer's credit score is under a statutory minimum
The note contains any balloon payment
questions.explanation: Under CBS section 4.7.1, when the seller carries financing the contract is conditional on the seller finding the buyer's payments, interest rate, terms, cost, and legal compliance satisfactory — and the seller may terminate by the Seller or Private Financing Deadline if not. No statute sets a minimum credit score or bars balloon payments in seller financing.

questions.question_text: A buyer wants to change the closing date proposed in the seller's counterproposal. The buyer should submit:
distractors: A verbal request to the listing broker
An amendment to the original offer
A new offer replacing the original
questions.explanation: When modifying terms in a counterproposal, the buyer submits another counterproposal using the Counterproposal Form.
